full_prompt: Design one long-span steel roof bay for a community dance center where the long-span space must provide a column-free dance floor at least 24.4 m (80 ft) by 18.3 m (60 ft) with a clear height of 9.1 m (30 ft) beneath a steel roof. The structure must be steel-primary using ASTM A992 wide-flange members (Fy 345 MPa, Fu 450 MPa) for primary girders and columns and ASTM A500 Grade C HSS (Fy 345 MPa) for lateral bracing, supporting a lightweight metal-deck-plus-concrete-topping roof. The bay must carry simultaneously a roof dead load of 1.2 kPa from the metal deck, insulation, and roofing; a roof live load of 0.96 kPa per IBC; a snow load of 1.2 kPa using a reference climate mapped as ground snow 1.44 kPa via ASCE 7-22 flat-roof conversion; a wind uplift of 1.0 kPa on the roof per ASCE 7-22 components and cladding; and a lateral wind of 1.0 kPa on the long elevation applied as equivalent static pressure. All pass/fail criteria must be satisfied: (R1) every primary girder, column, and brace must achieve AISC 360-22 demand-to-capacity ratio at or below 1.0 under ASCE 7-22 LRFD combinations, (R2) mid-span roof girder deflection under 1.0 dead plus 1.0 live must not exceed span/240 which is 76.2 mm for the 18.3 m short direction, (R3) inter-story lateral drift at the roof under wind must not exceed H/400 which is 22.8 mm, (R4) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis of the primary long-span girder under 1.0 dead plus 1.0 snow must yield a first-mode load factor of at least 2.0 against lateral-torsional buckling, and (R5) the total structural steel tonnage in the column-free bay must not exceed 12.0 metric tons as an efficiency target. Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with a cut list of wide-flange and HSS sections, plus a structural report with AISC 360-22 DCR maxima, mid-span deflection, lateral drift, buckling load factor, and total steel tonnage.
short_prompt:
Design one long-span steel roof bay for a community dance center providing a column-free 24.4 x 18.3 m (80 x 60 ft) floor with 9.1 m (30 ft) clear height. Primary girders and columns are ASTM A992 wide-flange; lateral bracing is ASTM A500 Grade C HSS; the roof is lightweight metal deck plus concrete topping. Design per AISC 360-22 under ASCE 7-22 LRFD combinations (DCR <= 1.0 on every primary member and brace).

Load cases applied simultaneously.
- Roof dead 1.2 kPa (deck, insulation, roofing).
- Roof live 0.96 kPa (IBC).
- Snow 1.2 kPa (ASCE 7-22 flat-roof from 1.44 kPa ground).
- Roof wind uplift 1.0 kPa (ASCE 7-22 C&C).
- Lateral wind 1.0 kPa on the long elevation (equivalent static).
Also require midspan girder deflection under 1.0D+1.0L <= span/240 (76.2 mm on the 18.3 m short direction), roof drift under wind <= H/400 (22.8 mm), a linear-buckling factor >= 2.0 on the primary long-span girder under 1.0D+1.0S against LTB, and total structural steel tonnage <= 12.0 t.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: AISC 360-22 Chapter F/H member checks (LTB with Cb, HSS P-M, shear Vn) are spreadsheet closed-form on top of FEA demands.

prompt.title: Long-span steel roof bay for community dance center
prompt.brief: Design a 24.4 m x 18.3 m column-free steel roof bay with 9.1 m clear height, meeting AISC 360-22 DCR, deflection, drift, LTB buckling, and tonnage targets.

material.HSS_braces: ASTM A500 Grade C Fy 345 MPa

load_cases.name: snow
load_cases.derivation: 1.44 kPa ground to flat-roof per ASCE 7-22

objective.primary: pass all AISC 360-22 DCR and serviceability limits

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam for girders/columns, truss for braces, shell for deck

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal expansion handled by expansion joints, not FEA.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Connection design per AISC 360-22 Chapter J
Acoustic performance of the dance floor

eval_coverage: AISC 360-22 DCR closed-form check over linear FEA
